title: GI Embryology: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of GI Embryology
Identify key diagnostic features of GI Embryology
Apply clinical reasoning to GI Embryology management
Recognize common pitfalls in GI Embryology

sections.title: Overview of GI Embryology
sections.content:
**GI Embryology**

Foregut, midgut, hindgut derivatives; midgut rotation; vitelline duct; common GI malformations

**Key Points:**
• Foregut: esophagus to proximal duodenum (to ampulla of Vater), liver, gallbladder, pancreas
• Midgut: distal duodenum to proximal 2/3 transverse colon — supplied by SMA
• Hindgut: distal 1/3 transverse colon to upper rectum — supplied by IMA

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Foregut: esophagus to proximal duodenum (to ampulla of Vater), liver, gallbladder, pancreas
• Midgut: distal duodenum to proximal 2/3 transverse colon — supplied by SMA

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Hindgut: distal 1/3 transverse colon to upper rectum — supplied by IMA
• Midgut rotation: 270° counterclockwise around SMA; malrotation → Ladd bands → volvulus
• Bilious emesis in neonate = malrotation with volvulus until proven otherwise (surgical emergency)

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for GI Embryology:**

• Hindgut: distal 1/3 transverse colon to upper rectum — supplied by IMA
• Midgut rotation: 270° counterclockwise around SMA; malrotation → Ladd bands → volvulus
• Bilious emesis in neonate = malrotation with volvulus until proven otherwise (surgical emergency)

mcqs.question: Which of the following is MOST characteristic of GI Embryology?

options.text: Foregut: esophagus to proximal duodenum (to ampulla of Vater), liver, gallbladder, pancreas
options.explanation: Foregut: esophagus to proximal duodenum (to ampulla of Vater), liver, gallbladder, pancreas

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases

mcqs.question: What is the KEY diagnostic or management consideration for GI Embryology?

options.text: Bilious emesis in neonate = malrotation with volvulus until proven otherwise (surgical emergency)
options.explanation: Bilious emesis in neonate = malrotation with volvulus until proven otherwise (surgical emergency)

options.text: Immediate surgical intervention in all cases

mcqs.question: A patient presents with findings consistent with GI Embryology. Which finding would you expect?

options.text: Midgut: distal duodenum to proximal 2/3 transverse colon — supplied by SMA
options.explanation: Midgut: distal duodenum to proximal 2/3 transverse colon — supplied by SMA
